title: RedoxRRI
text: RedoxRRI formalises holobiont resilience coordinated redox buffering across plant, soil, microbial compartments. Rather analysing domain independently, framework models resilience emergent, cross-domain property can expressed either scalar index (RRI) compositional allocation simplex space. RedoxRRI provides transparent extensible framework quantify holobiont redox resilience integrating three complementary domains: Plant physiology (oxidative / nitrosative buffering stress traits) Soil redox stability (Eh redox-coupled chemistry) Microbial resilience Microbial resilience, represented single blended domain can incorporate: microbial abundance functional composition (micro_data) microbial network organization (igraph network metrics) primary output package : per-sample Redox Resilience Index (RRI) scaled [0,1]) ternary-ready compositional representation (Physio, Soil, Micro) row sums equal 1 optional system-level index stored attr(x, “RRI_index”)

title: RedoxRRI
text: domain (Physiology, Soil, Microbial) first summarized one-dimensional latent score per sample using user-selected method (e.g., PCA, FA, UMAP, WGCNA). latent scores scaled range [0, 1]. Redox Resilience Index (RRI) computed weighted linear combination scaled domain scores. domain weights user-defined must sum one. Higher RRI values indicate samples stronger physiological buffering, greater soil redox stability, higher microbial resilience relative samples dataset. Optional coupling terms can included capture coherence among domains, allowing index reflect domain magnitudes also agreement joint stability.

what: Microbial domain as a blended score
title: RedoxRRI
text: preserve three-part (Physiology-Soil-Microbial) ternary representation, microbial resilience modeled single blended domain score. microbial abundance (functional composition) data microbial network data available, microbial domain score computed weighted blend two components. parameter alpha_micro controls relative contribution: alpha_micro = 1: abundance functional composition alpha_micro = 0: network structure alpha_micro = 0.5: equal contribution (default) design allows flexible emphasis microbial composition, microbial organization, , maintaining consistent three-domain representation suitable compositional ternary visualization.

title: RedoxRRI
text: data used vignette fully simulated provided solely demonstration testing purposes. generated using simulate_redox_holobiont() mimics qualitative properties redox-ecological systems, including: correlated plant physiological traits soil redox gradients stability microbial functional heterogeneity spatial grouping temporal structure simulation represent real experiment, species, site, ecosystem.

title: RedoxRRI
text: analyses vignette fully reproducible rely simulated data generated within package. simulated object contains: ROS_flux: plant physiological traits (samples × variables) Eh_stability: soil redox variables micro_data: microbial functional features id: sample metadata (spatial temporal structure) graph (optional): igraph network list networks
code: library(dplyr) ##  ## Attaching package: 'dplyr' ## The following objects are masked from 'package:stats': ##  ##     filter, lag ## The following objects are masked from 'package:base': ##  ##     intersect, setdiff, setequal, union library(tibble) if (!requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) {   message("Optional package 'patchwork' not installed.") } set.seed(1342) sim <- simulate_redox_holobiont() # sim <- simulate_redox_holobiont(seed = 1)  str(sim, max.level = 1) ## List of 6 ##  $ id          :'data.frame':    600 obs. of  4 variables: ##  $ ROS_flux    :'data.frame':    600 obs. of  4 variables: ##  $ Eh_stability:'data.frame':    600 obs. of  5 variables: ##  $ micro_data  :'data.frame':    600 obs. of  60 variables: ##  $ latent_truth: num [1:600] 0.705 0.804 0.849 0.674 0.445 ... ##  $ graph       : NULL

title: RedoxRRI
text: Resistance = immediate deviation redox potential disturbance Resilience = rate magnitude redox recovery
code: if (!requireNamespace("FactoMineR", quietly = TRUE)) {   knitr::opts_chunk$set(eval = FALSE) }  res <- rri_pipeline_st(   ROS_flux = sim$ROS_flux,   Eh_stability = sim$Eh_stability,   micro_data = sim$micro_data,   id = sim$id,   direction_phys = "auto",   direction_anchor_phys = "FvFm",   direction_soil = "auto",   direction_anchor_soil = "Eh" )

title: RedoxRRI
text: plot requires suggested packages: ggtern, ggplot2, viridis. Choosing latent-dimension methods Different domains may justify different latent representations: physiology: pca, fa soil: pca, wgcna (co-regulated redox syndromes expected) microbial abundance: pca, nmf, wgcna nonlinear regimes: umap illustrate alternative specifications suggested packages available.
code: if (requireNamespace("ggtern", quietly = TRUE) &&   requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE) &&   requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE) &&   requireNamespace("viridis", quietly = TRUE)) {     pt1<- plot_RRI_ternary(res$row_scores_comp)    } else {   message("Install ggtern, ggplot2, and viridis to enable ternary plotting.") } ## Warning in ggplot2::geom_point(data = centroid, ggplot2::aes(x = .data$Physio, ## : Ignoring unknown aesthetics: z specA <- res  if (requireNamespace("WGCNA", quietly = TRUE)) {   specA <- rri_pipeline_st(     ROS_flux     = sim$ROS_flux,     Eh_stability = sim$Eh_stability,     micro_data   = sim$micro_data,     graph        = sim$graph,     alpha_micro  = 0.6,     method_phys  = "pca",     method_soil  = "wgcna",     method_micro = "pca"   )   specA$meta$rri_index } else {   message("WGCNA not installed; skipping example.") } ##    Power SFT.R.sq slope truncated.R.sq  mean.k. median.k.  max.k. ## 1      1  0.00558 -6.69       -0.22900 2.190000  2.140000 2.59000 ## 2      2  0.01700 -5.89       -0.22900 1.240000  1.190000 1.70000 ## 3      3  0.05330 -7.32       -0.17300 0.725000  0.681000 1.12000 ## 4      4  0.06360 -6.14       -0.16500 0.437000  0.402000 0.74200 ## 5      5  0.07340 -5.40       -0.15600 0.270000  0.244000 0.49600 ## 6      6  0.08250 -4.89       -0.14600 0.171000  0.151000 0.33300 ## 7      7  0.09110 -4.50       -0.13700 0.110000  0.096000 0.22500 ## 8      8  0.14100 -5.20       -0.06090 0.071700  0.061800 0.15300 ## 9      9  0.14900 -4.82       -0.05540 0.047400  0.040300 0.10500 ## 10    10  0.15500 -4.49       -0.05040 0.031700  0.026500 0.07170 ## 11    12  0.16500 -3.96       -0.04140 0.014500  0.011800 0.03410 ## 12    14  0.02940 -2.06       -0.05740 0.006830  0.005350 0.01640 ## 13    16  0.05160 -2.53       -0.01710 0.003270  0.002460 0.00797 ## 14    18  0.06030 -2.52        0.00605 0.001580  0.001140 0.00390 ## 15    20  0.06890 -2.50        0.02590 0.000776  0.000531 0.00192 ## [1] 0.3167483 specB <- res  if (requireNamespace("uwot", quietly = TRUE)) {   specB <- rri_pipeline_st(     ROS_flux     = sim$ROS_flux,     Eh_stability = sim$Eh_stability,     micro_data   = sim$micro_data,     method_phys  = "umap",     method_soil  = "umap",     method_micro = "pca"   )   specB$meta$rri_index } else {   message("uwot not installed; skipping example.") } ## [1] 0.4077453

what: Second example: compositional geometry and temporal resilience
title: RedoxRRI
text: example, illustrate three advanced features RedoxRRI: Compositional projection using clr geometry covariance-based compensation term Rolling temporal resilience dynamics specification closely reflects longitudinal ecological data resilience evaluated time rather single snapshot.
code: set.seed(91202)  res_adv <- rri_pipeline_st(   ROS_flux        = sim$ROS_flux,   Eh_stability    = sim$Eh_stability,   micro_data      = sim$micro_data,   id              = sim$id,   time_col        = "time",   group_cols      = c("plot", "depth"),   mode            = "rolling",   window          = 4,   comp_space      = "clr",   add_compensation = TRUE,   compensation_weight = 0.2,   direction_phys  = "auto",   direction_anchor_phys = "FvFm",   direction_soil  = "auto",   direction_anchor_soil = "Eh" )  head(res_adv$row_scores_comp) ##      Physio      Soil        Micro       RRI ## 1 0.5221394 0.4587786 1.908198e-02 0.7487045 ## 2 0.4111453 0.5888547 6.222230e-09 0.6874197 ## 3 0.4379477 0.5024814 5.957090e-02 0.4486399 ## 4 0.3980691 0.5285082 7.342264e-02 0.8333942 ## 5 0.4547408 0.4672573 7.800193e-02 0.6265479 ## 6 0.4784418 0.4630530 5.850514e-02 0.7512837 range(rowSums(res_adv$row_scores_comp[, c("Physio","Soil","Micro")])) ## [1] 1 1 if (requireNamespace("ggtern", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE) &&     requireNamespace("viridis", quietly = TRUE)) {    plot_RRI_ternary(     res_adv$row_scores_comp,     centroid_method = "auto"   )  } else {   message("Install ggtern, ggplot2, and viridis to enable ternary plotting.") } ## Warning in ggplot2::geom_point(data = centroid, ggplot2::aes(x = .data$Physio, ## : Ignoring unknown aesthetics: z

previous_headings: Second example: compositional geometry and temporal resilience
title: RedoxRRI
text: direction_phys = “lower_is_better” direction_soil = “lower_is_better” direction_micro = “higher_is_better”
code: set.seed(47162)  sim <- simulate_redox_holobiont(   seed = 47162,   n_plot = 4,   n_depth = 4,   n_plant = 4,   n_time = 50,   p_micro = 240,   disturbance_strength = 0.6,   decoupling = 0.6,   zero_inflation = 0.4,   MNAR_strength = 0.5,   stochastic_reassembly = TRUE ) res_main <- rri_pipeline_st(   ROS_flux = sim$ROS_flux,   Eh_stability = sim$Eh_stability,   micro_data = sim$micro_data,   id = sim$id,   time_col = "time",   group_cols = c("plot", "depth", "plant_id"),   mode = "snapshot",   reducer = "mfa",   scaling = "pnorm",   comp_space = "clr",   add_compensation = TRUE,   compensation_weight = 0.2,   direction_phys = "lower_is_better",   direction_soil = "lower_is_better",   direction_micro = "higher_is_better" )  df_val <- tibble::tibble(   latent = sim$latent_truth,   rri = res_main$row_scores$RRI,   time = sim$id$time,   plot = sim$id$plot,   depth = sim$id$depth,   plant_id = sim$id$plant_id )  cor_complete <- function(x, y, method = "pearson") {   stats::cor(x, y, use = "complete.obs", method = method) }  n_obs <- nrow(df_val) cor_val <- cor_complete(df_val$latent, df_val$rri) lm_fit <- stats::lm(rri ~ latent, data = df_val) r_sq <- summary(lm_fit)$r.squared  stat_label <- paste0(   "r = ", round(cor_val, 2),   ", R² = ", round(r_sq, 2),   ", n = ", n_obs )  p2A <- ggplot2::ggplot(df_val, ggplot2::aes(x = latent, y = rri)) +   ggplot2::geom_point(alpha = 0.25, size = 1.1) +   ggplot2::geom_smooth(     method = "lm",     se = TRUE,     color = "#B2182B",     linewidth = 0.9   ) +   ggplot2::annotate(     "text",     x = min(df_val$latent, na.rm = TRUE),     y = max(df_val$rri, na.rm = TRUE),     hjust = 0,     vjust = 1,     size = 3.2,     label = stat_label   ) +   ggplot2::labs(     x = "True latent redox state",     y = "Redox Resilience Index (RRI)"   ) +   theme_ems()  df_ts <- df_val |>   dplyr::group_by(time) |>   dplyr::summarise(     latent_mean = mean(latent, na.rm = TRUE),     rri_mean = mean(rri, na.rm = TRUE),     rri_se = stats::sd(rri, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(dplyr::n()),     .groups = "drop"   ) |>   dplyr::mutate(     rri_lo = rri_mean - 1.96 * rri_se,     rri_hi = rri_mean + 1.96 * rri_se   )  p2B <- ggplot2::ggplot(df_ts, ggplot2::aes(x = time)) +   ggplot2::geom_line(     ggplot2::aes(y = latent_mean),     color = "grey35",     linewidth = 1   ) +   ggplot2::geom_ribbon(     ggplot2::aes(ymin = rri_lo, ymax = rri_hi),     fill = "#2C7BB6",     alpha = 0.18   ) +   ggplot2::geom_line(     ggplot2::aes(y = rri_mean),     color = "#2C7BB6",     linewidth = 1.2   ) +   ggplot2::labs(     x = "Time",     y = "Scaled value (0–1)"   ) +   theme_ems()  p2 <- p2A + p2B +   patchwork::plot_annotation(     title = "Validation of latent recovery and temporal tracking",     tag_levels = "A"   ) p2 ## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

previous_headings: Second example: compositional geometry and temporal resilience
title: RedoxRRI
text: ###Structural consequences cross-domain decoupling understand cross-domain coupling contributes holobiont stability, simulate systems increasing levels domain decoupling quantify three structural properties resulting RRI decomposition: Mean pairwise correlation among domains Net covariance contribution aggregate variance Cross-domain coordination strength Higher decoupling reduce coordination among domains therefore expect metrics decline increasing decoupling.  ####Single-domain indicators vs integrated RRI plot × depth × plant_id × time  ###RRI plotting
code: library(RedoxRRI) library(dplyr) library(tibble) library(purrr) library(patchwork) library(ggplot2)  scale01 <- function(x) {   rng <- range(x, na.rm = TRUE)   if (diff(rng) == 0) {     return(rep(0.5, length(x)))   }   (x - rng[1]) / diff(rng) }  cor_complete <- function(x, y, method = "spearman") {   stats::cor(x, y, use = "complete.obs", method = method) }  # Simulate one reference dataset   set.seed(4716254)  sim <- simulate_redox_holobiont(   seed = 4716254,   n_plot = 4,   n_depth = 4,   n_plant = 4,   n_time = 50,   p_micro = 240,   disturbance_strength = 0.6,   decoupling = 0.6,   zero_inflation = 0.4,   MNAR_strength = 0.5,   stochastic_reassembly = TRUE )  # MFA-based RRI    res_mfa <- rri_pipeline_st(   ROS_flux = sim$ROS_flux,   Eh_stability = sim$Eh_stability,   micro_data = sim$micro_data,   id = sim$id,   time_col = "time",   group_cols = c("plot", "depth", "plant_id"),   mode = "snapshot",   reducer = "mfa",   scaling = "pnorm",   comp_space = "clr",   add_compensation = TRUE,   compensation_weight = 0.2,   direction_phys = "lower_is_better",   direction_soil = "lower_is_better",   direction_micro = "higher_is_better" )  # PCA / per-domain RRI    res_pca <- rri_pipeline_st(   ROS_flux = sim$ROS_flux,   Eh_stability = sim$Eh_stability,   micro_data = sim$micro_data,   id = sim$id,   time_col = "time",   group_cols = c("plot", "depth", "plant_id"),   mode = "snapshot",   reducer = "per_domain",   scaling = "pnorm",   comp_space = "clr",   add_compensation = TRUE,   compensation_weight = 0.2,   direction_phys = "lower_is_better",   direction_soil = "lower_is_better",   direction_micro = "higher_is_better" )  # Prepare comparison dataframe    df_fig3 <- tibble(   rri_pca = scale01(res_pca$row_scores$RRI),   rri_mfa = scale01(res_mfa$row_scores$RRI) )  rho <- cor_complete(df_fig3$rri_pca, df_fig3$rri_mfa, method = "spearman")  # Figure 3    fig3 <- ggplot(df_fig3, aes(x = rri_pca, y = rri_mfa)) +   geom_point(alpha = 0.18, size = 1.2, color = "grey30") +   stat_density_2d(     color = "grey65",     linewidth = 0.6   ) +   geom_abline(     slope = 1,     intercept = 0,     linetype = "dashed",     color = "black",     linewidth = 0.8   ) +   geom_smooth(     method = "lm",     se = FALSE,     color = "#2C7FB8",     linewidth = 1.1   ) +   annotate(     "text",     x = 0.02,     y = 0.96,     hjust = 0,     vjust = 1,     size = 5,     label = paste0("Spearman \u03c1 = ", round(rho, 3))   ) +   coord_equal(xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1), expand = FALSE) +   labs(     x = "RRI (PCA reduction)",     y = "RRI (MFA reduction)"   ) +   theme_classic(base_size = 13)  fig3 ## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x' library(patchwork) library(RedoxRRI) library(dplyr) library(tidyr) library(tibble) library(purrr)  # decoupling gradient dec_grid <- seq(0.1, 0.8, by = 0.1)  # number of stochastic replicates n_rep <- 50   compute_structure <- function(decoupling, rep){    sim <- simulate_redox_holobiont(     seed = 1000 + rep + round(decoupling * 100),     decoupling = decoupling   )    res <- rri_pipeline_st(     ROS_flux = sim$ROS_flux,     Eh_stability = sim$Eh_stability,     micro_data = sim$micro_data,     reducer = "mfa",     scaling = "pnorm",     comp_space = "clr",     add_compensation = TRUE,     compensation_weight = 0.2   )    domains <- res$row_scores[, c("Physio","Soil","Micro")]    # standardize domains   domains <- scale(domains)    # Panel A — synchrony   cor_mat <- cor(domains)   mean_corr <- mean(cor_mat[upper.tri(cor_mat)])    # Panel B — domain divergence   domain_divergence <- mean(apply(domains, 1, sd))    # Panel C — system instability   rri_instability <- mean(abs(diff(res$row_scores$RRI)), na.rm = TRUE)    tibble(     decoupling = decoupling,     mean_corr = mean_corr,     domain_divergence = domain_divergence,     rri_instability = rri_instability   ) }  # run simulations df <- tidyr::expand_grid(   decoupling = dec_grid,   rep = 1:n_rep ) |>   pmap_dfr(~compute_structure(..1, ..2))    # rename to avoid summarise masking bug df <- df |> rename(mean_corr_raw = mean_corr)    # summarise statistics df_summary <- df |>   group_by(decoupling) |>   summarise(      mean_corr = mean(mean_corr_raw),     corr_se = sd(mean_corr_raw) / sqrt(n()),      div_mean = mean(domain_divergence),     div_se = sd(domain_divergence) / sqrt(n()),      instab_mean = mean(rri_instability),     instab_se = sd(rri_instability) / sqrt(n()),      .groups = "drop"   )    # Panel A — domain synchrony pA <- ggplot(df_summary, aes(decoupling, mean_corr)) +    geom_ribbon(     aes(ymin = mean_corr - corr_se,         ymax = mean_corr + corr_se),     fill = "#3b78a8",     alpha = 0.25   ) +    geom_line(color = "#3b78a8", linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(color = "#3b78a8", size = 2) +    labs(     x = "Simulated cross-domain decoupling",     y = "Mean pairwise domain correlation"   ) +    theme_classic()    # Panel B — domain divergence pB <- ggplot(df_summary, aes(decoupling, div_mean)) +    geom_ribbon(     aes(ymin = div_mean - div_se,         ymax = div_mean + div_se),     fill = "#d55e00",     alpha = 0.25   ) +    geom_line(color = "#d55e00", linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(color = "#d55e00", size = 2) +    labs(     x = "Simulated cross-domain decoupling",     y = "Cross-domain divergence"   ) +    theme_classic()    # Panel C — system instability pC <- ggplot(df_summary, aes(decoupling, instab_mean)) +    geom_ribbon(     aes(ymin = instab_mean - instab_se,         ymax = instab_mean + instab_se),     fill = "#7570b3",     alpha = 0.25   ) +    geom_line(color = "#7570b3", linewidth = 1) +   geom_point(color = "#7570b3", size = 2) +    labs(     x = "Simulated cross-domain decoupling",     y = "RRI fluctuation magnitude (system instability)"   ) +    theme_classic()    # combine panels fig4 <- (pA | pB | pC) +   plot_annotation(     title = "Structural consequences of cross-domain decoupling",     subtitle = "Increasing decoupling reduces synchrony while increasing divergence and systemic instability",     tag_levels = "A"   )  fig4 if (requireNamespace("dplyr", quietly = TRUE)) library(dplyr) if (requireNamespace("tibble", quietly = TRUE)) library(tibble) if (requireNamespace("patchwork", quietly = TRUE)) library(patchwork) if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) library(ggplot2)     # ---- Helpers ---- scale01 <- function(x) {   x <- as.numeric(x)   r <- range(x, na.rm = TRUE)   if (!all(is.finite(r)) || diff(r) == 0) return(rep(0.5, length(x)))   (x - r[1]) / diff(r) }  pick_col <- function(df, patterns, df_name = deparse(substitute(df))) {   stopifnot(is.data.frame(df))   nm <- names(df)   hit <- which(Reduce(`|`, lapply(patterns, function(p) grepl(p, nm, ignore.case = TRUE))))   if (length(hit) == 0) {     stop(       "Could not find a matching column in ", df_name, ".\n",       "Tried patterns: ", paste(patterns, collapse = ", "), "\n",       "Available columns: ", paste(nm, collapse = ", ")     )   }   if (length(hit) > 1) {     # Prefer exact-ish matches if present     exact <- hit[grepl(paste0("^(", paste(patterns, collapse = "|"), ")$"), nm[hit], ignore.case = TRUE)]     if (length(exact) >= 1) hit <- exact[1] else hit <- hit[1]   }   nm[hit] }  micro_indicator_pc1 <- function(micro_data) {   if (is.null(micro_data)) return(rep(NA_real_, 0))   micro_data <- as.data.frame(micro_data)      # numeric matrix   X <- suppressWarnings(as.matrix(micro_data))   storage.mode(X) <- "double"      # handle all-NA columns   keep <- colSums(is.finite(X)) > 0   X <- X[, keep, drop = FALSE]   if (ncol(X) == 0) return(rep(NA_real_, nrow(micro_data)))      # stabilize counts/abundances   X <- log1p(pmax(X, 0))      # remove zero-variance columns   sds <- apply(X, 2, stats::sd, na.rm = TRUE)   X <- X[, is.finite(sds) & sds > 0, drop = FALSE]   if (ncol(X) == 0) return(rep(NA_real_, nrow(micro_data)))      pc <- stats::prcomp(X, center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)   as.numeric(pc$x[, 1]) }  make_panel <- function(df, y_col, y_lab, tag, add_identity = TRUE) {   r_val <- stats::cor(df$latent, df[[y_col]], use = "complete.obs", method = "pearson")      p <- ggplot(df, aes(x = latent, y = .data[[y_col]])) +     geom_point(alpha = 0.22, size = 0.9, color = "black") +     geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "#B2182B", linewidth = 0.9) +     {if (isTRUE(add_identity)) geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50")} +     annotate(       "label",       x = 0.02, y = 0.98,       hjust = 0, vjust = 1,       size = 3.2,       label = paste0("Pearson r = ", format(round(r_val, 3), nsmall = 3)),       fill = "white",       label.size = 0.2     ) +     coord_cartesian(xlim = c(0, 1), ylim = c(0, 1)) +     labs(x = "Latent redox state (scaled 0–1)", y = y_lab) +     theme_ems() +     theme(       plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),       axis.title = element_text(size = 11),       axis.text = element_text(size = 10)     ) +     labs(tag = tag)      p }   # Build dataframe     # Soil: prefer "Eh" if present; otherwise "eh" substring eh_col <- pick_col(sim$Eh_stability, patterns = c("Eh", "eh", "redox"), df_name = "sim$Eh_stability")  # Plant: prefer "FvFm", "Fv/Fm", then any photochemical efficiency-like pattern fvfm_col <- pick_col(   sim$ROS_flux,   patterns = c("FvFm", "Fv/Fm", "fvfm", "photochem", "PSII"),   df_name = "sim$ROS_flux" )  micro_pc1 <- micro_indicator_pc1(sim$micro_data)  df_6 <- tibble(   latent = scale01(sim$latent_truth),   eh = scale01(sim$Eh_stability[[eh_col]]),   fvfm = scale01(sim$ROS_flux[[fvfm_col]]),   micro_pc1 = scale01(micro_pc1),   rri = scale01(res_main$row_scores$RRI) )  # Panels   pA <- make_panel(df_6, "eh", paste0("Soil Eh (", eh_col, "; scaled 0–1)"), tag = "A") ## Warning in annotate("label", x = 0.02, y = 0.98, hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = ## 3.2, : Ignoring unknown parameters: `label.size` pB <- make_panel(df_6, "fvfm", paste0("Plant Fv/Fm (", fvfm_col, "; scaled 0–1)"), tag = "B") ## Warning in annotate("label", x = 0.02, y = 0.98, hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = ## 3.2, : Ignoring unknown parameters: `label.size` pC <- make_panel(df_6, "micro_pc1", "Microbiome indicator (PC1 of micro_data; scaled 0–1)", tag = "C") ## Warning in annotate("label", x = 0.02, y = 0.98, hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = ## 3.2, : Ignoring unknown parameters: `label.size` pD <- make_panel(df_6, "rri", "Redox Resilience Index (RRI; scaled 0–1)", tag = "D") ## Warning in annotate("label", x = 0.02, y = 0.98, hjust = 0, vjust = 1, size = ## 3.2, : Ignoring unknown parameters: `label.size` fig_6 <- (pA | pB) / (pC | pD) +   plot_annotation(     title = "Single-domain indicators versus integrated RRI",     subtitle = "All variables scaled to [0,1]; dashed line indicates 1:1 identity; red line shows linear fit ± 95% CI.",     theme = theme(       plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14), previous_headings: Second example: compositional geometry and temporal resilience
title: RedoxRRI
text: ternary representation expresses relative allocation holobiont redox buffering among plant physiology, soil chemistry, microbial resilience. comp_space = "clr" used, compositional geometry follows Aitchison principles, centroid computed clr space available. ensures geometric coherence avoids spurious averaging simplex space. optional compensation term increases RRI negative cross-domain covariance detected, reflecting compensatory dynamics among domains.

title: RedoxRRI
code: res_w1 <- rri_pipeline_st(   ROS_flux = sim$ROS_flux,   Eh_stability = sim$Eh_stability,   micro_data = sim$micro_data,   w1 = 0.6, w2 = 0.25, w3 = 0.15 )  res_w1$meta$rri_index ## [1] 0.45387

title: RedoxRRI
code: ## Validation utilities ### 1. Latent recovery    rri_latent_correlation(res_w1, sim$latent_truth) ## [1] -0.9417382 ## Compensation strength rri_compensation_index(res_w1) ## [1] 0.3186399 ## Sensitivity of RRI rankings to aggregation weights  sens <- rri_sensitivity(res_w1)  sens ##   weight_physio weight_soil weight_micro spearman_rank_correlation ## 1           0.2        0.40         0.40                 0.5834016 ## 2           0.3        0.35         0.35                 0.8886380 ## 3           0.4        0.30         0.30                 0.9795185 ## 4           0.5        0.25         0.25                 0.9963836 ## 5           0.6        0.20         0.20                 0.9983571

title: RedoxRRI
text: RRI rankings remain highly stable (>0.9 Spearman correlation) moderate perturbations domain weighting, indicating structural robustness aggregation scheme.
code: if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {      library(ggplot2)      ggplot(sens,          aes(x = weight_physio,              y = spearman_rank_correlation)) +          # Stability reference band     geom_hline(yintercept = 0.9,                linetype = "dashed",                linewidth = 0.6,                colour = "grey40") +          # Smooth curve     geom_line(linewidth = 1.2, colour = "#3B0F70") +          # Points     geom_point(size = 3.5,                shape = 21,                fill = "#FDE725",                colour = "black",                stroke = 0.6) +          # Tight limits     scale_y_continuous(       limits = c(min(0.85, min(sens$spearman_rank_correlation, na.rm = TRUE)),                  1),       expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02))     ) +          scale_x_continuous(       expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.02))     ) +          labs(       title = "Robustness of RRI to Domain Weight Perturbation",       subtitle = "Spearman rank stability relative to baseline configuration",       x = "Weight assigned to Physiology domain",       y = "Spearman rank correlation"     ) +          theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +          theme(       plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 15),       plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, margin = margin(b = 10)),       axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),       panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),       panel.grid.major.x = element_blank()     )    } else {   message("Install ggplot2 to visualize sensitivity results.") }

title: RedoxRRI
text: recommended workflow: Assemble domain matrices samples rows consistent ordering. Choose latent methods appropriate domain. Decide microbial blending parameter alpha_micro. Sensitivity-test domain weights methods. Validate RRI independent outcomes.

text: Quantifying holobiont resilience coordinated redox buffering across plant, soil, microbial domains.

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index for Integrative Stress Biology
text: RedoxRRI R package computing visualizing Holobiont Redox Resilience Index (RRI) integrating plant physiology, soil redox chemistry, microbial resilience unified, directionally identifiable framework. package designed applications redox ecology, plant–soil–microbe interactions, holobiont resilience research, explicit support spatial structure, temporal dynamics, statistical challenges typical ecological data.

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index for Integrative Stress Biology
text: Plant physiological stress buffering (e.g. ROS-related traits) Soil redox chemistry electron-acceptor stability proxies Microbial resilience derived abundance data /ecological networks

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index for Integrative Stress Biology
text: Dimension reduction via PCA, FA, NMF, WGCNA, related approaches Explicit sign control optional anchor variables ensure reproducibility

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index for Integrative Stress Biology
text: Median imputation within spatial strata Tolerance MNAR missingness, zero inflation, collinearity

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index for Integrative Stress Biology
text: Absolute domain scores, scaled ([0,1]), suitable statistical modeling Compositional domain contributions, summing 1, designed ternary visualization

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index for Integrative Stress Biology
text: Redox resilience holobiont scale emerges interacting biological subsystems, rather single measurable trait. RedoxRRI formalizes concept quantifying three domains: Physio — plant oxidative buffering stress response Soil — redox chemistry electron-acceptor stability Micro — microbial functional capacity /network resilience domain summarized using latent variable derived multivariate indicators. Domain scores explicitly oriented higher values consistently indicate greater resilience, scaled ([0,1]), integrated composite Redox Resilience Index (RRI).

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index for Integrative Stress Biology
text: Modular — domains can added, removed, reweighted Transparent — transformations inspectable reproducible Flexible — supports multiple dimension-reduction strategies Mechanistic — designed inference, black-box prediction

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index for Integrative Stress Biology
text: Principal Component Analysis (PCA) Factor analysis Nonlinear embeddings (e.g. UMAP) Network-based summaries (e.g. WGCNA)

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index for Integrative Stress Biology
text: Abundance- function-based representations Optional network topology metrics derived ecological graphs

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index for Integrative Stress Biology
text: User-defined weighting across biological domains Optional coupling terms capture cross-domain coherence

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index for Integrative Stress Biology
text: Prepare domain-specific data plant physiological traits soil redox chemical indicators microbial abundance, functional profiles, networks Derive latent scores domain Integrate domains composite Redox Resilience Index Evaluate compare alternative weighting schemes stress disturbance scenarios ecological experimental contexts

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index for Integrative Stress Biology
code: install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("mghotbi/RedoxRRI")  # with vignettes install.packages("remotes") install.packages("BiocManager")  BiocManager::install(c(   "BiocStyle",   "rmarkdown",   "knitr"))  remotes::install_github(   "mghotbi/RedoxRRI",   build_vignettes = TRUE,   dependencies = TRUE)

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index for Integrative Stress Biology
text: Conceptual overview RedoxRRI framework. Multivariate indicators plant physiology, soil redox chemistry, microbial systems summarized domain-level latent scores, directionally aligned, scaled, integrated unified holobiont-scale Redox Resilience Index (RRI).
code: library(RedoxRRI)  # Simulate a holobiont redox system sim <- simulate_redox_holobiont(seed = 1) str(sim)  # Compute the Redox Resilience Index res <- rri_pipeline_st(   ROS_flux     = sim$ROS_flux,   Eh_stability = sim$Eh_stability,   micro_data   = sim$micro_data,   id           = sim$id,    # Direction anchoring    direction_phys = "auto",   direction_anchor_phys = "FvFm",   direction_soil = "auto",   direction_anchor_soil = "Eh" )  # Absolute domain scores (for analysis) head(res$row_scores)  # Compositional domain contributions (for ternary) head(res$row_scores_comp)  # Ternary visualization plot_RRI_ternary(res$row_scores_comp)

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index for Integrative Stress Biology
text: primary output object class RRI, containing: row_scores Absolute domain scores per-sample RRI (scaled [0,1]) row_scores_comp Compositional domain contributions (Physio + Soil + Micro = 1) RRI meta Metadata including model settings system-level RRI index (rri_index) compositional scores directly suitable ternary visualization.

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index for Integrative Stress Biology
text: RedoxRRI designed hypothesis-driven analysis stress resilience complex biological systems, focus interpretability mechanistic insight. package particularly well suited : Ecological environmental research Quantifying resilience across abiotic stress gradients Integrative stress biology Soil–plant–microbiome interaction studies Comparative sensitivity analyses Method development exploratory modeling

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index for Integrative Stress Biology
text: RedoxRRI black-box predictive model. Instead, prioritizes: explicit modeling choices traceable transformations raw data index values comparison alternative hypotheses rather automated optimization makes RedoxRRI especially suitable research contexts understanding resilience changes important measuring much.

what: Ternary Plot of Holobiont Redox Resilience Allocation — plot_RRI_ternary
title: Ternary Plot of Holobiont Redox Resilience Allocation — plot_RRI_ternary
text: Creates ternary diagram visualising compositional allocation holobiont redox buffering capacity across plant physiology (Physio), soil redox chemistry (Soil), microbial resilience (Micro). Points filled according per-sample RRI values.

title: Ternary Plot of Holobiont Redox Resilience Allocation — plot_RRI_ternary
code: plot_RRI_ternary(   ternary_df,   point_size = 5,   point_alpha = 0.9,   palette = "plasma",   show_subtitle = TRUE,   show_centroid = TRUE,   centroid_shape = 23,   centroid_size = 1.4,   tolerance = 1e-06,   renormalize = FALSE,   centroid_method = c("auto", "simplex_mean", "aitchison_mean") )

title: Ternary Plot of Holobiont Redox Resilience Allocation — plot_RRI_ternary
text: ternary_df data frame containing compositional columns Physio, Soil, Micro, RRI. point_size Numeric; size ternary points. point_alpha Numeric 0 1 controlling point transparency. palette Character; viridis palette option. show_subtitle Logical; display system-level RRI mean subtitle. show_centroid Logical; add compositional centroid marker. centroid_shape Numeric; ggplot2 shape centroid marker. centroid_size Numeric multiplier centroid size. tolerance Numeric; tolerance used compositional closure checks. renormalize Logical; TRUE, renormalises rows sum 1. centroid_method Character; one "auto", "simplex_mean", "aitchison_mean".

title: Ternary Plot of Holobiont Redox Resilience Allocation — plot_RRI_ternary
text: ggtern object.

title: Ternary Plot of Holobiont Redox Resilience Allocation — plot_RRI_ternary
text: clr-transformed coordinates attached attribute ("clr"), centroid can computed using Aitchison mean, ensuring geometric coherence compositional space. Otherwise, simplex arithmetic mean used.

title: Ternary Plot of Holobiont Redox Resilience Allocation — plot_RRI_ternary
code: # ---- Simulate holobiont dataset (snapshot) ---- sim <- simulate_redox_holobiont(   n_plot = 20,   n_depth = 30,   n_plant = 8,   n_time = 10,   p_micro = 30,   seed = 1234 )  # ---- Compute RedoxRRI ---- res <- rri_pipeline_st(   ROS_flux = sim$ROS_flux,   Eh_stability = sim$Eh_stability,   micro_data = sim$micro_data,   id = sim$id,   reducer = "per_domain",   scaling = "pnorm" )  # ---- Extract compositional scores ---- ternary_df <- res$row_scores_comp  # ---- Plot ternary allocation ---- p <- plot_RRI_ternary(   ternary_df,   point_size = 3,   show_centroid = TRUE ) #> Warning: Ignoring unknown aesthetics: z

title: Example workflow for RedoxRRI — redoxrri_example
text: Demonstrates complete workflow computing Holobiont Redox Resilience Index (RRI) using simulated plant, soil, microbiome data.

title: Example workflow for RedoxRRI — redoxrri_example
text: example generates fully synthetic dataset using simulate_redox_holobiont computes Redox Resilience Index using rri_pipeline_st. simulation framework represents simplified soil–plant–microbiome system includes: Disturbance forcing time Partial plant–soil–microbiome decoupling pH–Eh interaction within soil redox domain Zero-inflated high-dimensional microbial abundance data Optional stochastic microbial reassembly microbial association network generated using igraph simulated data represent real biological system provided solely demonstration purposes.

title: Example workflow for RedoxRRI — redoxrri_example
Cross-Domain Compensation Index — rri_compensation_index
text: Computes scale-invariant cross-domain compensation index based mean pairwise correlations among Physio, Soil, Micro domain scores.

title: Cross-Domain Compensation Index — rri_compensation_index
code: rri_compensation_index(res)

title: Cross-Domain Compensation Index — rri_compensation_index
text: res object returned rri_pipeline_st().

title: Cross-Domain Compensation Index — rri_compensation_index
text: single numeric compensation index \([-1, 1]\). Positive values indicate compensatory dynamics (negative cross-domain correlations), negative values indicate synchrony.

title: Cross-Domain Compensation Index — rri_compensation_index
text: compensation index defined : $$   \mathrm{Comp} = -\frac{2}{k(k-1)} \sum_{<j} \mathrm{Cor}(D_i, D_j) $$ \(k = 3\) \(D_i\) domain-level scores. Using correlations rather covariances ensures scale invariance boundedness across datasets.

title: Cross-Domain Compensation Index — rri_compensation_index
code: # ---- Simulate small holobiont dataset ---- sim <- simulate_redox_holobiont(   n_plot = 2,   n_depth = 1,   n_plant = 2,   n_time = 8,   p_micro = 20,   seed = 1 )  # ---- Compute RedoxRRI ---- res <- rri_pipeline_st(   ROS_flux = sim$ROS_flux,   Eh_stability = sim$Eh_stability,   micro_data = sim$micro_data,   id = sim$id,   reducer = "per_domain",   scaling = "pnorm" )  # ---- Compute cross-domain compensation index ---- comp <- rri_compensation_index(res)

what: Latent Recovery Correlation for RedoxRRI — rri_latent_correlation
title: Latent Recovery Correlation for RedoxRRI — rri_latent_correlation
text: Computes Pearson correlation per-sample Redox Resilience Index (RRI) known latent truth vector. function intended simulation-based validation interpreted predictive accuracy.

title: Latent Recovery Correlation for RedoxRRI — rri_latent_correlation
code: rri_latent_correlation(   res,   latent_truth,   method = c("pearson", "spearman", "kendall") )

title: Latent Recovery Correlation for RedoxRRI — rri_latent_correlation
text: res object returned rri_pipeline_st(). latent_truth Numeric vector true latent values. method Correlation method. One "pearson", "spearman", "kendall".

title: Latent Recovery Correlation for RedoxRRI — rri_latent_correlation
text: single numeric correlation coefficient.

title: Latent Recovery Correlation for RedoxRRI — rri_latent_correlation
text: function designed simulation benchmarking. empirical datasets, latent truth exists metric used.

what: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index (RRI) with Spatio-Temporal Dynamics — rri_pipeline_st
title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index (RRI) with Spatio-Temporal Dynamics — rri_pipeline_st
text: Computes holobiont-level Redox Resilience Index (RRI) integrating plant physiological traits, soil redox chemistry, microbial resilience unified, directionally identifiable index. framework supports static (snapshot), rolling-window, event-based resilience modes, optional compositional geometry (clr), multiblock reduction via MFA, covariance-based compensation.

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index (RRI) with Spatio-Temporal Dynamics — rri_pipeline_st
code: rri_pipeline_st(   ROS_flux,   Eh_stability,   micro_data = NULL,   graph = NULL,   id = NULL,   time_col = NULL,   group_cols = NULL,   mode = c("snapshot", "rolling", "event"),   window = 3,   align = c("right", "center", "left"),   event_col = NULL,   baseline_label = "pre",   recovery_labels = "recovery",   alpha_micro = 0.5,   method_phys = "pca",   method_soil = "pca",   method_micro = "pca",   direction_phys = c("auto", "higher_is_better", "lower_is_better"),   direction_soil = c("auto", "higher_is_better", "lower_is_better"),   direction_micro = c("auto", "higher_is_better", "lower_is_better"),   direction_anchor_phys = NULL,   direction_anchor_soil = NULL,   direction_anchor_micro = NULL,   scale_by = NULL,   network_agg = c("equation", "mean"),   w1 = 0.4,   w2 = 0.35,   w3 = 0.25,   add_coupling = FALSE,   coupling_weight = 0,   coupling_fun = c("geometric_mean", "agreement"),   norm_method = NULL,   reducer = c("per_domain", "mfa"),   scaling = c("minmax_legacy", "pnorm"),   comp_space = c("closure_legacy", "clr"),   ref_stats = NULL,   add_compensation = FALSE,   compensation_weight = 0 )

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index (RRI) with Spatio-Temporal Dynamics — rri_pipeline_st
text: ROS_flux Data frame plant physiological variables (rows = samples). Eh_stability Data frame soil redox chemistry variables (rows = samples). micro_data Optional data frame microbial abundance functional features. graph Optional igraph object list igraph objects representing microbial network structure. id Optional data frame describing experimental design (number rows inputs). time_col Optional character. Name time column id. group_cols Optional character vector grouping variables id. mode Character. One "snapshot", "rolling", "event". window Integer >= 2. Rolling window size (mode = "rolling"). align Character. Alignment rule rolling window: "right", "center", "left". event_col Optional character. Column id identifying event phases. baseline_label Character. Label identifying baseline phase. recovery_labels Character vector identifying recovery phases. alpha_micro Numeric 0 1 controlling blending microbial abundance network components. method_phys Character. Reduction method plant block. method_soil Character. Reduction method soil block. method_micro Character. Reduction method microbial block. direction_phys Character. Orientation rule plant latent dimension. direction_soil Character. Orientation rule soil latent dimension. direction_micro Character. Orientation rule microbial latent dimension. direction_anchor_phys Optional character. Anchor variable plant orientation. direction_anchor_soil Optional character. Anchor variable soil orientation. direction_anchor_micro Optional character. Anchor variable microbial orientation. scale_by Optional character vector grouping variables used scaling. network_agg Character. Network aggregation method: "equation" "mean". w1 Numeric weight plant domain. w2 Numeric weight soil domain. w3 Numeric weight microbial domain. Must sum w1 w2 1. add_coupling Logical. TRUE, adds cross-domain coherence term. coupling_weight Numeric 0 1 controlling weight coupling term. coupling_fun Character. Coupling function: "geometric_mean" "agreement". norm_method Optional character. provided, overrides block-specific methods. reducer Character. Reduction strategy: "per_domain" "mfa". scaling Character. Scaling rule: "minmax_legacy" "pnorm". comp_space Character. Compositional projection method: "closure_legacy" "clr". ref_stats Optional list reference statistics used scaling. add_compensation Logical. TRUE, includes covariance-based compensation term. compensation_weight Numeric 0 1 controlling compensation weight.

title: Holobiont Redox Resilience Index (RRI) with Spatio-Temporal Dynamics — rri_pipeline_st
text: list class "RRI" containing: row_scores: Raw domain RRI values. row_scores_comp: Compositional domain scores RRI. dyn_scores: Dynamic resilience metrics (applicable). meta: Metadata describing model configuration.

title: Sensitivity Analysis for RedoxRRI — rri_sensitivity
text: function perturbs aggregation step recompute latent domain scores. therefore evaluates stability domain integration rather reducer sensitivity.

title: Sensitivity Analysis for RedoxRRI — rri_sensitivity
code: # ---- Simulate a small holobiont dataset ---- sim <- simulate_redox_holobiont(   n_plot = 2,   n_depth = 4,   n_plant = 2,   n_time = 8,   p_micro = 20,   seed = 1 )  # ---- Compute RedoxRRI ---- res <- rri_pipeline_st(   ROS_flux = sim$ROS_flux,   Eh_stability = sim$Eh_stability,   micro_data = sim$micro_data,   id = sim$id,   reducer = "per_domain",   scaling = "pnorm" )  # ---- Evaluate aggregation sensitivity ---- sens <- rri_sensitivity(   res,   weight_grid = seq(0.3, 0.5, by = 0.1) )

what: Simulate a Holobiont Redox Dataset with Spatio-Temporal Structure — simulate_redox_holobiont
title: Simulate a Holobiont Redox Dataset with Spatio-Temporal Structure — simulate_redox_holobiont
text: Generate synthetic plant–soil–microbiome dataset () latent redox state evolving time, (ii) mid-season disturbance pulse, (iii) partial cross-domain decoupling, (iv) configurable microbial sparsity (zero inflation), (v) missing---random (MNAR) Eh dropout strongly reduced states. simulator designed benchmarking, validation, demonstration RedoxRRI workflows. intended represent real ecosystem.

title: Simulate a Holobiont Redox Dataset with Spatio-Temporal Structure — simulate_redox_holobiont
code: simulate_redox_holobiont(   n_plot = 4,   n_depth = 2,   n_plant = 5,   n_time = 15,   p_micro = 60,   seed = 123,   include_graph = FALSE,   depth_labels = NULL,   disturbance_strength = 0.4,   disturbance_center = NULL,   disturbance_width = 0.1,   seasonal_amp = 0.08,   seasonal_phase = 0,   stochastic_reassembly = TRUE,   decoupling = 0.3,   zero_inflation = 0.4,   MNAR_strength = 0.4,   Eh_dropout_threshold = 0.25,   micro_mean = 8,   micro_slope = 3,   micro_lambda_min = 1e-08,   micro_lambda_max = 1e+06 )

title: Simulate a Holobiont Redox Dataset with Spatio-Temporal Structure — simulate_redox_holobiont
text: n_plot Integer >= 1. Number plots. n_depth Integer >= 1. Number soil depth strata. n_plant Integer >= 1. Number plants per plot–depth. n_time Integer >= 2. Number time points per plant. p_micro Integer >= 1. Number microbial features. seed Optional integer seed. Use NULL stochastic runs. include_graph Logical. TRUE igraph available, return simulated network graph. depth_labels Optional character vector length n_depth. NULL, depths labeled "D1", "D2", .... disturbance_strength Numeric [0, 1]. Magnitude disturbance pulse. disturbance_center Optional numeric. NULL, centered median time. disturbance_width Numeric > 0. Pulse width fraction season length. seasonal_amp Numeric >= 0. Amplitude seasonal forcing. seasonal_phase Numeric. Phase shift seasonal sine wave (radians). stochastic_reassembly Logical. TRUE, microbial counts include stochastic reassembly component (mixture intensities). decoupling Numeric [0, 1]. Cross-domain decoupling (0=tightly coupled). zero_inflation Numeric [0, 1]. Probability microbial entry set 0. MNAR_strength Numeric [0, 1]. Strength MNAR Eh dropout reduced states. Eh_dropout_threshold Numeric [0, 1]. Latent threshold dropout can occur. micro_mean Numeric > 0. Baseline mean intensity microbial counts. micro_slope Numeric >= 0. Coupling strength latent redox state microbial intensity. micro_lambda_min Numeric > 0. Lower bound microbial Poisson intensity. micro_lambda_max Numeric > 0. Upper bound microbial Poisson intensity.

title: Simulate a Holobiont Redox Dataset with Spatio-Temporal Structure — simulate_redox_holobiont
text: named list : id: Data frame experimental design (plot, depth, plant_id, time). ROS_flux: Plant physiological indicators (data frame). Eh_stability: Soil redox chemistry variables (data frame; Eh may include NA). micro_data: Microbial feature matrix (data frame; non-negative integers). latent_truth: Underlying latent redox state [0, 1]. graph: Optional igraph object requested available.

title: Simulate a Holobiont Redox Dataset with Spatio-Temporal Structure — simulate_redox_holobiont
code: # ---- Simulate small holobiont dataset ---- sim <- simulate_redox_holobiont(   n_plot = 2,   n_depth = 1,   n_plant = 2,   n_time = 8,   p_micro = 20,   seed = 1 )  # ---- Compute RedoxRRI ---- res <- rri_pipeline_st(   ROS_flux = sim$ROS_flux,   Eh_stability = sim$Eh_stability,   micro_data = sim$micro_data,   id = sim$id,   reducer = "per_domain",   scaling = "pnorm" )  # ---- Correlation with known latent truth (simulation only) ---- cor_val <- rri_latent_correlation(   res,   latent_truth = sim$latent_truth,   method = "pearson" )
